description: A Monte Carlo result is reported as precise when it is actually undersampled and the estimate has not converged.
body: Failure mode: the run stops at a fixed sample count without checking the standard error, so a high-variance estimator returns a point estimate whose confidence interval is still wide. Detection signals: estimates that shift noticeably when the run is repeated, a standard error far above the claimed precision, and the interval not shrinking as expected. Do not report a point estimate without its confidence interval. Safer fallback: compute the standard error, draw more samples until the interval meets the target, and apply variance reduction if convergence is too slow.
